description: Hakkasan Abu Dhabi is the capital's most celebrated fine dining restaurant — a one-Michelin-star Cantonese institution occupying 16,000 square feet within the iconic Emirates Palace Mandarin Oriental, with a dramatic standalone entrance, sultry carved-wood interiors designed by Gilles & Boissier, and a sweeping 108-seat terrace overlooking the palace grounds and city skyline. One of the group's finest global outposts, Hakkasan Abu Dhabi has held its Michelin star across every edition of the guide and consistently delivers impeccable Cantonese cuisine, from Peking duck with caviar to jasmine tea-smoked wagyu ribs. The combination of the world's most lavish hotel setting and globally acclaimed culinary standards makes this the defining luxury dining address in Abu Dhabi.
highlights: One Michelin star retained since 2022; Emirates Palace Mandarin Oriental; 16,000 sq ft Gilles & Boissier-designed space; sweeping terrace; Peking duck with caviar; celebrity clientele.
restaurant_setting: Luxury hotel dining room with terrace
sources: Michelin Guide Abu Dhabi 2026; Gault & Millau UAE; Time Out Abu Dhabi; The National; 50 Best Discovery

description: Talea by Antonio Guida is Abu Dhabi's most refined Italian restaurant — a one-Michelin-star dining room at the Emirates Palace Mandarin Oriental that brings the celebrated cucina di famiglia philosophy of Milan's legendary chef to the UAE capital. Led by Guida's protégé Luigi Stinga, winner of the inaugural Michelin Guide Abu Dhabi's Young Chef Award, the restaurant celebrates handmade pasta, daily-baked bread, house-made cheese, and produce from the hotel's own kitchen garden. The airy, contemporary dining room with its frescoed walls and terrace tables against the Arabian Gulf is one of Abu Dhabi's most consistently praised luxury dining experiences.
highlights: One Michelin star; Antonio Guida's cucina di famiglia; chef Luigi Stinga; Emirates Palace setting; house-made pasta and kitchen garden produce; terrace over the Gulf.
restaurant_setting: Luxury hotel dining room with terrace
sources: Michelin Guide Abu Dhabi 2026; 50 Best Discovery; Gault & Millau UAE; Time Out Abu Dhabi; The National

description: Erth is a landmark of global gastronomy — the world's first Michelin-starred Emirati restaurant, set within the historic Qasr Al Hosn cultural site in the heart of Abu Dhabi, where ancient stone walls and modern polished-concrete interiors designed by BONE Studio frame a menu that brings traditional Emirati cuisine into the contemporary fine dining conversation. Chef Debi Prasad Rath's dishes — from modern lamb machboos to the iconic Liwa dates batheeta — are crafted using locally sourced ingredients from farms across Abu Dhabi, Al Ain, and Hatta. A historically significant and visually stunning dining experience, awarded the Time Out Abu Dhabi Restaurant of the Year.
highlights: One Michelin star; world's first Michelin-starred Emirati restaurant; Qasr Al Hosn cultural site; BONE Studio design; locally sourced Emirati ingredients; Time Out Restaurant of the Year.
restaurant_setting: Historic cultural site
sources: Michelin Guide Abu Dhabi 2026; Time Out Abu Dhabi; The National; Gault & Millau UAE; Visit Abu Dhabi

description: Zuma Abu Dhabi is the capital's most iconic and enduring Japanese restaurant — a Michelin Guide-selected dining destination that has anchored the luxury dining circuit at The Galleria on Al Maryah Island since 2014, with a robata grill, sushi counter, and sweeping waterfront views over the Arabian Gulf. Described by Time Out Abu Dhabi as carrying more prestige than any other restaurant in the city, Zuma delivers its globally acclaimed izakaya-style Japanese cuisine — from miso-marinated black cod to sweet potato robata — with flawless consistency across a slick, design-led interior that attracts Abu Dhabi's most discerning clientele. The Saturday brunch is one of the city's most coveted weekend events.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; Abu Dhabi institution since 2014; The Galleria Al Maryah Island with waterfront views; robata grill and sushi counter; cited as most prestigious restaurant in city; award-winning Saturday brunch.
restaurant_setting: Design-led dining room with waterfront views
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Time Out Abu Dhabi; Gault & Millau UAE; 50 Best Discovery; Condé Nast Traveler

description: La Petite Maison Abu Dhabi — universally known as LPM — is the capital's definitive French Mediterranean institution, a Michelin Guide-selected restaurant at The Galleria on Al Maryah Island where the Niçoise menu of salt-baked sea bass, marinated lamb cutlets, escargot, and house-made pasta has made it the go-to address for Abu Dhabi's business and media elite since opening. The flower-strewn terrace delivers authentic Côte d'Azur atmosphere over the waterfront, and the electric-red bar attracts a sophisticated after-work crowd. Spoken about in reverent tones by long-term residents and consistently cited among the city's best and most beloved restaurants.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; Abu Dhabi institution; Niçoise menu of shared plates; flower-strewn terrace; Al Maryah Island waterfront; beloved of business and media elite; famous weekend brunch.
restaurant_setting: Elegant dining room with waterfront terrace
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Time Out Abu Dhabi; Gault & Millau UAE; What's On; Harper's Bazaar Arabia

description: Bord Eau by Nicolas Isnard is Abu Dhabi's most compelling contemporary French fine dining restaurant — a Michelin Guide-selected destination at the Shangri-La Qaryat Al Beri that brings the world-travelled, boundary-crossing cuisine of celebrated Burgundy chef Nicolas Isnard to a spectacular high-ceilinged dining room with double-height arched windows, glittering chandeliers, and sweeping creek views. The two tasting menus — 'French Classics' and 'Travels Around the World' — showcase Isnard's extraordinary flavour combinations, from Gillardeau oysters with burned eggplant and Sturia Caviar to inventive meat and seafood compositions shaped by decades of global culinary exploration.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; chef Nicolas Isnard; 'Travels Around the World' tasting menu; Shangri-La creek views; double-height arched-window dining room; Gault & Millau acclaimed.
restaurant_setting: Luxury hotel dining room with creek views
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Gault & Millau UAE; 50 Best Discovery; Time Out Abu Dhabi; The National

description: Fouquet's Abu Dhabi is one of the world's most culturally compelling restaurant settings — the Abu Dhabi outpost of the legendary 1899 Champs-Elysées brasserie, set inside Jean Nouvel's extraordinary dome at the Louvre Abu Dhabi, with a panoramic terrace over the Arabian Gulf. The menu was created in collaboration with three-Michelin-star chef Pierre Gagnaire and reimagines classic Parisian brasserie cooking — from beef tartare to sole meunière — alongside a Marta speakeasy-style bar above the restaurant. A Michelin Guide-selected address that combines iconic culinary heritage, exceptional architecture, and one of Abu Dhabi's most magnificent outdoor settings.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; inside Louvre Abu Dhabi under Jean Nouvel's dome; Pierre Gagnaire menu; panoramic Gulf terrace; 1899 Parisian brasserie legacy; Marta speakeasy bar.
restaurant_setting: Iconic museum setting with Gulf terrace
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Time Out Abu Dhabi; Harper's Bazaar Arabia; Gault & Millau UAE; What's On

description: COYA Abu Dhabi is the capital's defining Peruvian-Latin social dining destination — a Michelin Guide-selected restaurant at The Galleria on Al Maryah Island where a dramatised Incan-inspired interior, live DJ sets, and a ceviches-and-anticuchos menu combine to create one of the city's most reliably electric evening atmospheres. Since opening in 2017, COYA transformed Abu Dhabi's food scene and has remained one of its most talked-about addresses, particularly for its coveted waterfront terrace and celebrated cocktail programme. A Four Seasons Group partnership amplifies the service standards.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; Four Seasons partner; Al Maryah Island waterfront terrace; live DJs; Incan-inspired decor; leading Peruvian-Latin dining; Abu Dhabi institution since 2017.
restaurant_setting: Design-led dining room with waterfront terrace
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Time Out Abu Dhabi; Gault & Millau UAE; Visit Abu Dhabi; Harper's Bazaar Arabia

description: Ray's Grill is Abu Dhabi's most dramatically positioned steakhouse — a Michelin Guide-selected and 2026 Michelin Service Award winner on the 63rd floor of Conrad Abu Dhabi Etihad Towers, with floor-to-ceiling windows delivering 360-degree views of the city and the Arabian Gulf. Prime American grain-fed, Australian Wagyu, and tableside tartare preparation make this one of the Gulf's most compelling premium steak experiences, and the combination of vertiginous views, exceptional service under award-winning manager Ricardo Bout, and the prestige of the Etihad Towers address makes Ray's Grill the go-to power dining address in the capital.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; 2026 Michelin Service Award; 63rd floor of Conrad Etihad Towers; 360-degree Gulf views; prime American and Australian Wagyu; power dining destination.
restaurant_setting: Skyline dining room on 63rd floor
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; The National; Gault & Millau UAE; Best Bites UAE; Time Out Abu Dhabi

description: Martabaan by Hemant Oberoi is Abu Dhabi's most illustrious Indian fine dining address — a Michelin Guide-selected restaurant at the Emirates Palace Mandarin Oriental where legendary chef Hemant Oberoi, who for four decades cooked for presidents and royalty as Grand Chef of the Taj Group, presents a contemporary-classic menu of reimagined Indian dishes in an intimate, opulent dining room with views of the Arabian Gulf Marina. The restaurant's tasting menu — Oberoi's greatest hits from Mumbai to San Diego — delivers extraordinary culinary depth in a setting of unrivalled prestige. One of the few Indian restaurants in the Gulf that genuinely competes with the world's finest.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; chef Hemant Oberoi cooked for Obama, Clinton, and royalty; Emirates Palace Mandarin Oriental; Gulf Marina views; contemporary classic Indian tasting menu; intimate private dining.
restaurant_setting: Luxury hotel dining room with Gulf views
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Gault & Millau UAE; Time Out Abu Dhabi; Harper's Bazaar Arabia; Visit Abu Dhabi

description: Strawfire by Ross Shonhan is Abu Dhabi's most exciting recent arrival in luxury dining — a Michelin Guide-selected and 2026 Michelin Exceptional Cocktail Award winner at the Emirates Palace Mandarin Oriental, where Australian chef Ross Shonhan's warayaki technique (cooking over burning straw in the tradition of Japan's Kochi Prefecture) produces intensely smoky and flavourful meat dishes alongside exceptional sushi, in a jaw-dropping interior featuring hand-painted silk panels and the world's largest synthetic Shimenawa rope. Best Bites UAE rates this among the city's very top addresses.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; 2026 Michelin Exceptional Cocktail Award; warayaki cooking over burning straw; Emirates Palace Mandarin Oriental; hand-painted silk interior; outstanding sushi and meats; organic garden-to-table ethos.
restaurant_setting: Luxury hotel dining room
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; What's On; Best Bites UAE; 50 Best Discovery; Time Out Abu Dhabi

description: Butcher & Still is Abu Dhabi's most design-celebrated steakhouse — a Michelin Guide-selected restaurant at the Four Seasons Hotel on Al Maryah Island whose 1920s Chicago Prohibition-era interiors of rich timber, leather, and warm amber lighting have won multiple design awards and made it the most photographed restaurant in the city. Chef Marshall Allan Röth oversees an exceptional programme of prime US beef, from bone-in ribeye to sharing Chateaubriand, paired with an authentic Prohibition-era cocktail list that draws cocktail enthusiasts as much as steak lovers.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; Four Seasons Al Maryah Island; 1920s Chicago Prohibition interior; most design-awarded steakhouse in city; prime US cuts; acclaimed cocktail programme.
restaurant_setting: Design-led dining room in luxury hotel
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Time Out Abu Dhabi; Gault & Millau UAE; Harper's Bazaar Arabia
hotel_name_hint: Four Seasons Hotel Abu Dhabi at Al Maryah Island

description: 99 Sushi Bar Abu Dhabi is the capital's most established Japanese sushi destination — a refined restaurant on Al Maryah Island from Spanish brothers Pedro and Fernando de León whose meticulous sourcing of fish from Spain and Japan has set the standard for haute Japanese cuisine in the UAE capital. The restaurant features an intimate lounge, sushi bar, and a terrace with stunning views over the waterfront, and while the brand lost its Michelin star in the 2026 guide, it remains one of the most critically respected and high-spending Japanese dining addresses in Abu Dhabi.
highlights: Previously Michelin-starred; Spanish brothers' Japanese cuisine; Spanish and Japanese fish imports; intimate lounge and terrace; Al Maryah Island waterfront; benchmark Japanese sushi in Abu Dhabi.
restaurant_setting: Design-led dining room with waterfront terrace
sources: Michelin Guide 2022-2025 (1 star); Gault & Millau UAE; Time Out Abu Dhabi; Harper's Bazaar Arabia

description: Villa Toscana is Abu Dhabi's most acclaimed Italian seafood dining room — a Michelin Guide-selected and 2026 Michelin Young Chef Award recipient at The St. Regis Abu Dhabi Nation Towers, where chef Vittorio Nania delivers an immersive Tuscan, Umbrian, and Emilian feast of handmade pasta, premium Italian charcuterie imports, and expertly grilled seafood in a rustic aristocratic setting with Corniche views. The villa-inspired interior and dedicated focus on authentic regional Italian flavours make this one of the most charming and credible Italian dining rooms in the Gulf.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; 2026 Michelin Young Chef Award; chef Vittorio Nania; St. Regis Nation Towers; authentic Tuscan-Umbrian Italian; handmade pasta and Italian imports; Corniche views.
restaurant_setting: Luxury hotel dining room with Corniche views
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; The National; Time Out Abu Dhabi; Visit Abu Dhabi; Gault & Millau UAE
hotel_name_hint: The St. Regis Abu Dhabi Nation Towers

description: Catch by St. Regis is Abu Dhabi's most celebrated luxury seafood restaurant — a Michelin Guide-selected destination at The St. Regis Abu Dhabi that offers an extensive menu of caviar sturgeons, premium oysters, sushi, crab, and inventive seafood compositions prepared by chef Remon Nabil Hanna, who brings 17 years of international experience to one of the city's most refined and refined marine-centred dining rooms. The combination of St. Regis service standards, an exceptional raw bar programme, and the prestige of the Nation Towers address makes this the first choice for seafood connoisseurs in the capital.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; St. Regis Abu Dhabi Nation Towers; premium caviar, oysters, sushi and crab; chef Remon Nabil Hanna; exceptional raw bar programme; benchmark Abu Dhabi seafood.
restaurant_setting: Luxury hotel dining room
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Visit Abu Dhabi; Time Out Abu Dhabi; Harper's Bazaar Arabia
hotel_name_hint: The St. Regis Abu Dhabi Nation Towers

description: Dai Pai Dong is Abu Dhabi's most theatrically designed Cantonese restaurant — a Michelin Guide-selected destination at the Rosewood Abu Dhabi on Al Maryah Island that reimagines the hawker-stall culture of Hong Kong in a luxurious five-star setting of lacquered screens, dramatic lighting, and bespoke tableware. The kitchen sources local ingredients wherever possible — a rare commitment for Abu Dhabi's Chinese restaurants — and the Cantonese menu runs from dim sum and double-boiled broths to roasted duck and premium meat dishes. One of the capital's most distinctive and visually striking dining experiences.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; Rosewood Abu Dhabi; Hong Kong hawker-culture reimagined in luxury hotel; local ingredient sourcing; exceptional dim sum; dramatic lacquered interior.
restaurant_setting: Design-led dining room in luxury hotel
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Time Out Abu Dhabi; Gault & Millau UAE

description: Byblos Sur Mer is Abu Dhabi's most iconic Lebanese fine dining address — a Michelin Guide-selected restaurant at the InterContinental Abu Dhabi with a two-storey setting overlooking the marina and panoramic views of the Abu Dhabi skyline. An authentic and expansive Lebanese menu running from mezzeh and raw kibbeh to charcoal-grilled meats and fresh seafood, served in an atmosphere that attracts the city's Lebanese diaspora and international guests alike, has made Byblos Sur Mer one of the most enduring luxury dining addresses in the capital for decades.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; InterContinental Abu Dhabi; marina and skyline views; landmark Lebanese institution; extensive mezzeh and grill menu; panoramic two-storey waterfront setting.
restaurant_setting: Waterfront dining room with marina views
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Time Out Abu Dhabi; Harper's Bazaar Arabia; Visit Abu Dhabi

description: Li Beirut is Abu Dhabi's most acclaimed Lebanese fine dining restaurant — a Michelin Guide-selected destination at the Conrad Abu Dhabi Etihad Towers offering an elevated modern take on Levantine cuisine, from hummus with lamb and lobster kibbeh to intricate cold mezzeh and premium grilled dishes, with sweeping views of the Arabian Gulf from a dining room of refined elegance. One of the capital's most praised high-end Lebanese concepts and a consistent draw for international business travellers staying in the Etihad Towers complex.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; Conrad Abu Dhabi Etihad Towers; Gulf views; modern Lebanese fine dining; signature lobster kibbeh; consistently praised for quality and service.
restaurant_setting: Luxury hotel dining room with Gulf views
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Time Out Abu Dhabi; Gault & Millau UAE; ETIC Journal

description: Cafe Milano Abu Dhabi is the UAE capital's most fashionable Italian brasserie — a Michelin Guide-selected restaurant at the Four Seasons Hotel on Al Maryah Island that brings the legendary Georgetown (Washington DC) powerhouse dining room to Abu Dhabi, with a menu of refined classic Italian cuisine, a terrace over the waterfront, and an atmosphere that draws the city's business, diplomatic, and social elite. The combination of a globally recognized brand, Four Seasons service, and a Côte d'Azur-toned setting makes this one of the most sociable and prestigious Italian addresses in the city.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; Four Seasons Al Maryah Island; brand from Georgetown Washington DC powerhouse; refined Italian brasserie; waterfront terrace; elite business and social clientele.
restaurant_setting: Design-led dining room with waterfront terrace
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Time Out Abu Dhabi; Gault & Millau UAE
hotel_name_hint: Four Seasons Hotel Abu Dhabi at Al Maryah Island

description: Broadway is one of Abu Dhabi's most entertaining and sociable luxury dining rooms — a Michelin Guide-selected American brasserie at the Emirates Palace Mandarin Oriental where the combination of classic elevated comfort food (beef Wellington, escargot, signature desserts) with live entertainment by resident magicians and performers creates a uniquely theatrical experience in one of the world's most opulent hotel settings. One of very few restaurants in Abu Dhabi that combines serious culinary ambition with genuine entertainment spectacle, making it equally compelling for celebrations and business entertaining.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; Emirates Palace Mandarin Oriental; live entertainment and magic shows; elevated American comfort food; theatrical grand brasserie atmosphere; one of Abu Dhabi's most unique dining formats.
restaurant_setting: Grand hotel dining room with entertainment
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Time Out Abu Dhabi; Harper's Bazaar Arabia; What's On

description: Novikov Abu Dhabi is the UAE capital outpost of one of the world's most glamorous restaurant brands — a newly Michelin Guide-selected restaurant that brings the pan-Asian and Italian formula of Arkady Novikov's global empire to an Abu Dhabi setting of high-energy social dining, theatrical design, and a menu spanning Japanese, Asian, and Italian dishes across multiple formats. Novikov London is one of the world's most enduringly fashionable restaurants, and the Abu Dhabi debut has immediately established itself as one of the city's most hotly anticipated luxury openings.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected 2026; Novikov global brand; pan-Asian and Italian fusion; high-energy social dining; theatrical design; immediately fashionable Abu Dhabi arrival.
restaurant_setting: Design-led dining room
sources: Michelin Guide Selected 2026; Arab News; The National; What's On

description: Sand & Koal is Abu Dhabi's most romantically positioned beach dining experience — a newly Michelin Guide-selected seafood and grill restaurant on Mamsha Al Saadiyat with direct beachfront access and panoramic views of the Arabian Gulf from a setting that shifts from sun-drenched afternoon dining to DJ-soundtracked evening entertainment. The menu celebrates premium seafood — including a celebrated whole king crab in crustacean hollandaise — and live-fire grills in a contemporary, design-led beach club format that has quickly become one of the capital's most talked-about social destinations.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected 2026; Mamsha Al Saadiyat beachfront; whole king crab signature dish; DJ and live entertainment evenings; shifts from beach dining to buzzy social destination; exceptional sea views.
restaurant_setting: Beachfront dining room with terrace
sources: Michelin Guide Selected 2026; What's On; Arab News; The National

description: Em Sherif Sea Cafe is one of Abu Dhabi's most critically acclaimed new restaurants — a Michelin Guide-selected destination and 2025 Michelin Young Chef Award winner, located at the Rosewood Abu Dhabi on Al Maryah Island, where 28-year-old chef Yasmina Hayek continues her celebrated Lebanese family's culinary legacy in a contemporary waterfront seafood setting. The restaurant delivers an updated take on Lebanese seafood and mezze in an intimate, design-led room with waterfront views, combining Rosewood service standards with the authentic culinary heritage of the original Em Sherif restaurants in Beirut and Dubai.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; 2025 Michelin Young Chef Award winner; Rosewood Abu Dhabi; Lebanese seafood and mezze; chef Yasmina Hayek; waterfront Al Maryah Island setting.
restaurant_setting: Waterfront dining room in luxury hotel
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; The National; What's On; Time Out Abu Dhabi

description: Mika is Abu Dhabi's most acclaimed independently operated luxury dining destination and winner of the Time Out Abu Dhabi Restaurant of the Year award — a Michelin Bib Gourmand restaurant at Yas Marina in Yas Island where Italian chef Mario Loi (2025 Michelin Service Award winner) serves a seasonal European menu built on produce from his own kitchen garden and sustainably farmed local ingredients. The dreamy al fresco terrace over the marina is one of the most atmospheric dining settings in the UAE capital, and the combination of culinary ambition, genuine sustainability, and extraordinary waterfront atmosphere make this a compelling alternative to the city's hotel circuit.
highlights: Michelin Bib Gourmand; Time Out Abu Dhabi Restaurant of the Year; chef Mario Loi (2025 Michelin Service Award); own kitchen garden; Yas Marina terrace; sustainable fine dining with waterfront views.
restaurant_setting: Waterfront terrace on marina
sources: Michelin Bib Gourmand; Time Out Abu Dhabi; What's On; Gault & Millau UAE

description: 3Fils Abu Dhabi is the most anticipated luxury-casual Japanese-inspired dining opening of 2025 in the UAE capital — a Michelin Guide Opening of the Year award winner and Bib Gourmand recipient in the 2026 guide, located in Al Bateen Marina and sister to the legendary Dubai original that reinvented the city's seafront dining scene. Chef Jun Kimiyama's menu of high-quality sashimi, tostadas, and contemporary Japanese small plates in a relaxed, design-driven setting has immediately attracted Abu Dhabi's most discerning dining crowd. One of the first non-hotel luxury dining openings to earn instant critical acclaim in the capital.
highlights: Michelin Bib Gourmand and 2026 Opening of the Year; sister to legendary Dubai 3Fils; chef Jun Kimiyama; Al Bateen Marina waterfront; premium Japanese-inspired small plates; biggest new opening of 2025.
restaurant_setting: Waterfront dining room on marina
sources: Michelin Guide 2026 Opening of the Year; The National; Khaleej Times; What's On; Best Bites UAE
